mechanism_narrative: A falling FEDFUNDS rate signals an easing cycle that lowers discount rates and supports equity multiples, producing positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days when the policy path is clearly downward.
failure_condition: Reject if 20-day forward S&P 500 returns are not positive more than 55% of the time during sustained FEDFUNDS declines, or if the rolling 3-year Rank IC stays below 0.03.

mechanism_narrative: A rise in UNRATE above its recent trend indicates weakening household income and labor demand, which depresses earnings expectations and produces negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Reject if triggered observations deliver positive 20-day S&P 500 returns in more than 50% of cases, or if the signal's out-of-sample Rank IC is non-negative for any 24-month window.

mechanism_narrative: When RSAFS and HOUST both improve, it signals broad domestic demand stabilization and better revenue visibility, which should lift S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
candidate_trigger: RSAFS and HOUST both rise month-over-month
failure_condition: Reject if joint-positive RSAFS/HOUST months do not precede positive 60-day S&P 500 returns at least 60% of the time, or if either series alone fully explains the signal with no incremental interaction effect.

mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in INDPRO indicates output contraction and weaker corporate volume, which should pressure equity prices and generate negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Reject if two-month declines in INDPRO are followed by positive 20-day S&P 500 returns in more than 55% of cases, or if the rolling 3-year correlation between INDPRO changes and forward returns is near zero.

mechanism_narrative: An acceleration in CPIAUCSL without an offsetting policy easing raises real discount-rate pressure and profit-margin risk, causing negative S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
candidate_trigger: CPIAUCSL inflation trend rises while FEDFUNDS is unchanged or higher
failure_condition: Reject if inflation upshifts without policy easing do not coincide with negative 5-day S&P 500 returns in at least 55% of triggered instances, or if the sign flips positive during repeated inflation shocks.

mechanism_narrative: A decline in PAYEMS indicates payroll contraction and weakening income growth, which reduces expected sales and earnings and leads to negative S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Reject if three-month payroll declines are not followed by negative 60-day S&P 500 returns more than half the time, or if the signal loses all explanatory power after controlling for UNRATE.

mechanism_narrative: In a monetary tightening cycle, a high FEDFUNDS rate combined with slowing CPIAUCSL inflation is bearish for equities because restrictive policy persists while nominal growth cools, leading to negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: FEDFUNDS > 4.5% and CPIAUCSL inflation trend is falling
failure_condition: Reject if high-rate disinflation episodes do not produce negative 20-day S&P 500 returns at least 55% of the time, or if the signal performs no better than random during tightening cycles.

mechanism_narrative: A rebound in GDPC1 after a contractionary period signals improving aggregate demand and earnings prospects, which should generate positive S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
candidate_trigger: GDPC1 rises after at least one prior quarterly decline
failure_condition: Reject if post-decline GDP rebounds are not followed by positive 60-day S&P 500 returns in more than 55% of cases, or if the effect disappears once recession-state observations are removed.
